evalDetails.reasoning: The model correctly converts all callback-based functions to async/await, preserves all behavior including console.log order, identical error messages, return type, and sequential execution. The delay() helper nicely preserves the simulated async timing behavior. validateRecords correctly returns an object instead of multiple callback args. All 8 requirements are satisfied. The main weakness is the unconventional readFile error handling: instead of wrapping readFile in its own try/catch, it relies on string-matching in an outer catch block to differentiate readFile errors from other errors. This is functional but fragile — any unexpected error outside the inner try/catch blocks would be misclassified as a readFile error. The multiple nested inner try/catch blocks also add verbosity where a cleaner per-step pattern would be more idiomatic. No behavior differences from the original were found.
